subject: Databases
title: COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX
description: A 60-second lesson on COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX.

hook.text: How many users signed up this month? SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE created_at > '2024-01-01'.

buildup.text: Aggregate functions collapse many rows into one value. COUNT counts. SUM adds. AVG averages.

discovery.text: MIN and MAX find extremes. Oldest user? SELECT MIN(created_at) FROM users.

twist.text: COUNT(*) counts all rows. COUNT(email) counts only non-NULL emails. A subtle but important difference.

climax.text: Aggregates power dashboards, reports, and analytics. They're the backbone of data analysis in SQL.

punchline.text: Five functions. Endless insights. Master the aggregates.

quiz.question: What is the difference between COUNT(*) and COUNT(column)?
options: COUNT(*) counts all rows; COUNT(column) counts only rows where that column is not NULL
There is no difference between them
